entries.content: Score each candidate independently against the user prompt before comparing candidates to each other.
When the prompt contains explicit constraints, verify each candidate against every constraint instead of rewarding vague relevance.
Penalize factual or mathematical mistakes even if the answer sounds polished or confident.
Reward appropriate refusals only when the request is actually unsafe or disallowed; otherwise penalize unnecessary refusal.
For multiple valid answers, keep the scores of correct answers close together and reserve larger gaps for separating correct from incorrect responses.
Do not let verbosity, politeness, or formatting polish outweigh correctness, constraint satisfaction, and relevance.
